provenance.title: Quantifying the Risk-Return Tradeoff in Forecasting
affiliations: Université du Québec à Montréal — Département des Sciences Économiques
provenance.abstract: Average forecast accuracy is not the same as forecast reliability. I treat forecast loss differentials relative to a benchmark as a return series. These return series are then evaluated using risk-adjusted performance measures from finance, including the Sharpe ratio, Sortino ratio, Omega ratio, and drawdown-based metrics. I also introduce the Edge Ratio capturing...
key_claims: Forecast loss differentials can be treated as financial return series and evaluated with Sharpe, Sortino, Omega, and drawdown metrics.
The Survey of Professional Forecasters (SPF) dominates on risk-adjusted metrics despite being beatable on average RMSE, due to rarely exhibiting catastrophic failures.
The Edge Ratio — a novel metric — captures whether a model provides unique predictive information relative to the full forecasting frontier.
Risk-adjusted metrics extend naturally to meta-analyses across targets, horizons, and samples.
The Sharpe ratio equals the Diebold-Mariano t-statistic (up to a scaling constant) when loss differentials are serially uncorrelated.

architecture.primary_variant: Forecast Risk-Adjusted Evaluation Framework (no neural architecture — statistical/econometric framework)

modules.name: Benchmark Loss Differencer
modules.description: Computes r_t = L_benchmark_t - L_model_t for each evaluation period t
modules.input: Two scalar time series: L^B_t and L^M_t (loss values)

modules.name: Forecast Sharpe Ratio Calculator
modules.description: Computes mean return divided by standard deviation of returns

modules.name: Forecast Sortino Ratio Calculator
modules.description: Computes mean return divided by downside semi-deviation

modules.name: Forecast Omega Ratio Calculator
modules.description: Computes ratio of average upside to average absolute downside

modules.name: Maximum Drawdown Calculator
modules.description: Tracks worst cumulative underperformance from historical peak
modules.operation: Cumulative sum → running max → max drawdown

modules.name: Edge Ratio Calculator
modules.description: Computes how often a model reaches the forecasting frontier vs incurs regret
modules.input: Loss series for all M models {L_{j,t}} plus model under evaluation L_{M,t}
modules.operation: Frontier computation + Omega-style ratio with moving benchmark

modules.name: Meta-Analysis Aggregator
modules.description: Applies Sharpe/Sortino/Omega/Edge across cross-sectional design space (targets x horizons x samples)
modules.operation: Cross-sectional analogs of time-series risk metrics

modules.name: Forecasting Model Suite
modules.description: Collection of econometric/ML models: AR(4), FAAR, RR, KRR, RF, LGB, LGB+, LGBA+, NN, HNN, TabPFN, SPF
modules.input: FRED-QD panel data (248 quarterly U.S. macro/financial series)
modules.output: Point forecasts for GDP growth, CPI inflation, unemployment rate, housing starts

connections: FRED-QD Data → Forecasting Model Suite → {L^M_t, L^B_t}
{L_{j,t} for all j} → Edge Ratio Calculator → EdgeRatio

variants.name: Time-Series Risk Evaluation
variants.description: Applies metrics along the time axis for a single target/horizon combination

variants.name: Cross-Sectional Meta-Analysis
variants.description: Applies metrics across the design space (targets x horizons x evaluation windows)

mathematical_spec.name: Forecast Gain (Return)
mathematical_spec.role: Defines the 'return' of switching from benchmark B to model M at time t
hyperparameters: Loss function L (squared error, absolute error, log score, CRPS)

mathematical_spec.name: Average Return
mathematical_spec.role: Mean loss differential; corresponds to standard RMSE/MAE improvement

mathematical_spec.name: Forecast Sharpe Ratio
mathematical_spec.role: Average return per unit of total return volatility
mathematical_spec.role_in_pipeline: Primary risk-adjusted metric; equals DM t-stat / sqrt(T) when no serial correlation

mathematical_spec.name: Forecast Sortino Ratio
mathematical_spec.role: Average return per unit of downside volatility; penalizes underperformance only
mathematical_spec.role_in_pipeline: Primary asymmetric risk metric favored by paper

mathematical_spec.name: Forecast Omega Ratio
mathematical_spec.role: Ratio of average upside to average downside; non-parametric
mathematical_spec.role_in_pipeline: Full-distribution risk metric; Omega > 1 means more upside than downside

mathematical_spec.name: Maximum Drawdown
mathematical_spec.latex: \text{MaxDD} = \max_{1 \le t \le T} DD_t, \quad DD_t = M_t - R_t, \quad M_t = \max_{0 \le u \le t} R_u, \quad R_t = \sum_{s=1}^t r_s
mathematical_spec.role: Worst cumulative underperformance from historical peak
mathematical_spec.role_in_pipeline: Path-dependent downside risk; captures sustained failure episodes

mathematical_spec.name: Frontier Loss
mathematical_spec.role: Best competitor loss at time t (excluding model M)

mathematical_spec.name: Edge of Model M
mathematical_spec.role: Positive when M beats all competitors; negative when M lags frontier

mathematical_spec.name: Edge Ratio
mathematical_spec.role: Measures whether a model delivers unique predictive advantage relative to forecasting frontier
hyperparameters: M = total number of competing models
mathematical_spec.role_in_pipeline: Novel metric introduced by paper; null expectation ≈ 1 under equal performance

mathematical_spec.name: Meta-Analysis Percentage Return
mathematical_spec.role: Percentage improvement over benchmark across design-space index (target v, horizon h, sample s)

mathematical_spec.name: Meta-Analysis Sharpe
mathematical_spec.role: How reliably a model outperforms across forecasting problems

mathematical_spec.name: DM Statistic / Sharpe Link
mathematical_spec.role: Theoretical link between Sharpe ratio and Diebold-Mariano test statistic
mathematical_spec.role_in_pipeline: Connects framework to classical forecast evaluation tests

tensor_semantics.name: r_t
tensor_semantics.description: Scalar loss differential (return) at evaluation period t

tensor_semantics.name: R_t
tensor_semantics.description: Cumulative gain at time t: sum_{s=1}^t r_s

tensor_semantics.name: L_jt
tensor_semantics.description: Loss of model j at time t; matrix over all models and periods

tensor_semantics.name: e_Mt
tensor_semantics.description: Edge of model M at time t

tensor_semantics.name: R_meta
tensor_semantics.description: Cross-sectional return grid over design space

optimizer.name: Model-specific (see below); primary ML models use Adam or gradient boosting

lr_schedule.type: Early stopping (NN, LGB); None for linear models

data_augmentation: Blocked subsampling B=1000 bootstrap samples for HNN
training_pipeline.curriculum: Expanding window, re-estimated every 8 quarters (2 years)

notes.NN: 3 hidden layers, 400 neurons each, ReLU, dropout 0.2, Adam, early stopping
notes.HNN: 4 dedicated hemispheres + common core, 2 hidden layers x 400 neurons, ReLU, softplus variance, maximum likelihood training
notes.RF: 500 trees, 75% subsampling, min node size 5, random feature subsets
notes.LGB: Histogram-based gradient boosting, learning rate / depth / sampling fractions cross-validated with early stopping
notes.LGB+: Hybrid: tree-based + linear updates compete at each boosting step via OOB validation
notes.LGBA+: Alternating variant of LGB+, computationally lighter
notes.RR: Ridge regression, lambda selected by cross-validation
notes.KRR: Gaussian + Laplacian kernels, bandwidth sigma and lambda cross-validated
notes.FAAR: Factor-Augmented AR(4), 4 principal components from FRED-QD panel
notes.TabPFN: In-context learning, single forward pass, no task-specific training

datasets.name: FRED-QD
datasets.description: 248 quarterly U.S. macroeconomic and financial series, 1960Q1 onwards

datasets.name: Survey of Professional Forecasters (SPF)
datasets.description: Median consensus forecasts from professional forecasters, Philadelphia Fed

datasets.name: M4 Competition
datasets.description: 48,000 monthly time series (of 100,000 total), diverse domains

metrics.name: Forecast Omega Ratio
metrics.direction: higher is better, Omega>1 means net positive

baselines: Naive2 (M4 baseline: random walk with seasonal adjustment)
special_conditions: Expanding window estimation; models re-estimated every 8 quarters
2020 excluded from post-COVID evaluation to avoid COVID shock contamination
MARX transformation: 4 lags + moving averages of order 2, 4, 8
Series standardized to zero mean and unit variance over training sample
Direct forecasting at h=1, 2, 4 quarters ahead with separate models per horizon

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Loss function defaults to squared error (MSE) for primary analysis; MAE also reported
HAC adjustment NOT applied to Sharpe/Sortino/Omega ratios — they use sample (not long-run) variance
implementation_assumptions.basis: Explicitly stated as deliberate design choice

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Ridge regression lambda selected by time-series cross-validation (rolling/expanding fold)
implementation_assumptions.basis: Standard practice for macroeconomic forecasting; paper says 'cross-validated'

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Neural networks use standard normal weight initialization (He/Xavier for ReLU)
implementation_assumptions.basis: Not specified in paper; standard practice for ReLU networks

implementation_assumptions.assumption: FRED-QD transformations follow the McCracken-Ng (2020) transformation codes
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper references FRED-QD and stationarity transformation

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Edge Ratio denominator uses absolute value of negative edges (|e-_{M,t}|), not squared
implementation_assumptions.basis: Directly from Omega-ratio analogy described in paper

implementation_assumptions.assumption: TabPFN used with default pre-trained weights from Hollmann et al. (2022), no fine-tuning
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper describes in-context learning and no task-specific training

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Python implementation using scikit-learn, lightgbm, pytorch/tensorflow for models
implementation_assumptions.basis: Standard ML stack for these model families; not specified in paper

ambiguities.description: KRR kernel bandwidth sigma and lambda cross-validation scheme not fully specified (grid search range not given)
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Log-spaced grid search over [0.01, 100] for both sigma and lambda

ambiguities.description: LGB early stopping patience (number of rounds) not specified
Post-COVID end date varies across tables (2024Q2 vs 2025Q1) — slightly unclear which is canonical
ambiguities.primary_assumption: 2024Q2 for main text figures; appendix tables extend to 2025Q1

ambiguities.description: Whether the (M-1) scaling factor is applied using the fixed total M or a time-varying count of available models
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Fixed M = total number of models in the pool throughout
